intro:
There is no information available on the plant Arkezostis amazonica. However, I can suggest a similar plant, Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, also known as Arkezostis uva-ursi, or bearberry.

Arctostaphylos uva-ursi is a plant species that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae), and it is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to boreal forests.

The health benefits of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi include its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other urinary issues. It may also help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health.

The bioactive constituents of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi include arbutin, which is a glycoside with antiseptic and antiviral properties, and ursolic acid, which is a triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Arctostaphylos uva-ursi include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat urinary issues and other health problems.
